summary:
The mandibular branch (V3) of the trigeminal nerve provides motor innervation to the mylohyoid muscle, the anterior belly of the digastric muscle, and the tensor tympani. These are confirmed by multiple referenced sources as part of its known somatic motor distribution.  

(Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant facts directly related to neural innervation.)

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$

We must avoid any extraneous details or non-clinical commentary while ensuring accuracy based strictly upon established anatomical knowledge from cited references such as articles –30602_19, –29118_25, and –32553_10.
